2606.28900.title: MedEvoEval: Evaluating Continual Evolution of Doctor Agents through Simulated Clinical Episodes
2606.28900.paper_summary: Doctor agents are moving beyond single-turn answer generation toward evolving clinical decision systems. Within an outpatient episode, they acquire evidence, use examination and consultation resources, and decide when to finalize a diagnosis and management plan. Across episodes, their behavior may change through memory, retrieval, reflection, or other update mechanisms. Current evaluations only partially cover this setting. Fixed-input medical QA benchmarks score final answers from complete inputs, whereas many interactive benchmarks still focus on individual encounters or fixed runs, providing limited support for evaluating how episode-level decisions interact with cross-episode experience. We introduce MedEvoEval, an executable longitudinal evaluation framework based on action-gated simulated outpatient episodes. Each source case is converted into role-specific patient, examination, and manager views; evidence is revealed only through valid actions; and each episode records a structured trace that links observations, actions, final outputs, manager scores, and optional experience write-back. We release a runnable E&D artifact with 700 processed episodes, provenance notes, schemas, an episode runner, scoring scripts, configurations, example logs, analysis code, and trajectory- and step-level derivatives. Experiments show that episode traces expose process costs hidden by final-answer scoring, show how MDT-style consultation reallocates resources, and support longitudinal analyses of memory maturation, held-out transfer, update-stage response, and backward retention. Together, these results show that MedEvoEval provides a concrete basis for evaluating whether doctor agents improve through experience, transfer useful behavior, and retain earlier capabilities over time.
2606.28900.paper_summary_zh: 醫生代理正在從單次回答生成轉向發展臨床決策系統。在門診過程中，他們獲取證據，使用檢查和諮詢資源，並決定何時確定診斷和管理計劃。在不同的過程中，他們的行為可能通過記憶、檢索、反思或其他更新機制而改變。目前的評估僅部分涵蓋這種情境。固定輸入的醫療問答基準從完整輸入中評分最終答案，而許多互動基準仍然專注於個別事件或固定運行，對於評估事件級決策如何與跨事件經驗互動的支持有限。我們引入了MedEvoEval，一個基於行動閘控模擬門診過程的可執行縱向評估框架。每個源案例被轉換為角色特定的病人、檢查和管理者視圖；證據僅通過有效行動揭示；每個過程記錄一個結構化的痕跡，將觀察、行動、最終輸出、管理者分數和可選的經驗回寫聯繫起來。我們發布了一個可運行的E&D工件，包含700個處理過的過程、來源註釋、架構、過程運行器、評分腳本、配置、示例日誌、分析代碼以及軌跡和步驟級衍生物。實驗顯示，過程痕跡揭示了最終答案評分隱藏的過程成本，顯示了MDT風格的諮詢如何重新分配資源，並支持記憶成熟、保留轉移、更新階段反應和向後保留的縱向分析。綜合這些結果顯示，MedEvoEval為評估醫生代理是否通過經驗改善、轉移有用行為以及隨時間保留早期能力提供了具體基礎。
2606.28900.author: Hui Zhang et.al.
